name: MedAssist AI MVP
description: MVP implementation of MedAssist AI — a multi-agent medical AI platform built on OpenClaw with A2UI. Covers prerequisites, installation, project scaffolding, FastAPI backend, 7 specialist agents + coordinator, AI model wrappers, database setup, SKILL.md files, A2UI JSONL templates, and frontend API integration. Excludes auth, IaC, HIPAA encryption, and production deployment.

userStories.title: Install system prerequisites
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all system-level tools installed before any project work can begin. Install Node.js >= 22 (via nvm or official installer, verify with: node --version). Install Python >= 3.10 (verify with: python --version). Install Docker Desktop with Docker Compose (verify with: docker --version && docker compose version). Install Git (verify with: git --version). Install uv Python package manager (pip install uv or via installer, verify with: uv --version). Verify GPU availability if present (nvidia-smi on NVIDIA systems). All tools must be accessible from the terminal/command line.
acceptanceCriteria: node --version returns >= 22.x.x
python --version returns >= 3.10.x
docker --version returns a valid version and Docker daemon is running
docker compose version returns a valid version
git --version returns a valid version
uv --version returns a valid version
All commands are accessible from the system PATH
userStories.notes: These are manual installation steps. Verify each tool is installed and accessible.

userStories.title: Install and configure OpenClaw with LLM providers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need OpenClaw installed and configured with Claude API so the agent platform is ready. Run: npm install -g openclaw@latest. Run: openclaw onboard --install-daemon. Verify daemon running: openclaw doctor. Create/edit ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json with models config: default provider set to anthropic with model claude-sonnet-4-20250514 and apiKey from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var, local provider set to openai-compatible with baseUrl http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1 for LM Studio and contextWindow 32768. Verify config: openclaw health. Verify gateway accessible at ws://127.0.0.1:18789. Verify skills directory exists: ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/.
acceptanceCriteria: openclaw command is available globally (openclaw --version works)
openclaw doctor returns healthy status
~/.openclaw/openclaw.json exists with anthropic provider configured using claude-sonnet-4-20250514
~/.openclaw/openclaw.json has local provider entry for LM Studio at http://127.0.0.1:1234/v1
openclaw health returns successful status
~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ directory exists
OpenClaw gateway is reachable at ws://127.0.0.1:18789
userStories.notes: Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. LM Studio local provider is optional for MVP but config entry should exist.

userStories.title: Obtain API keys and configure environment secrets
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all external API keys obtained and stored so the backend can connect to cloud services. Obtain Anthropic API key (sk-ant-...) from console.anthropic.com — required for Claude API used by all agents. Obtain NCBI API key from ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account — required for PubMed searches by Research Agent (optional for dev, increases rate limit from 3/sec to 10/sec). Note DrugBank API key — required for Pharmacy Agent drug interaction lookups (can use free tier or mock for MVP). Store all keys securely — they will be placed in .env file during project setup (US-004). Do NOT commit keys to git.
acceptanceCriteria: Anthropic API key obtained and verified working (can make a test API call)
NCBI/PubMed API key obtained from ncbi.nlm.nih.gov account settings
DrugBank API access confirmed (API key or decision to use mock data for MVP)
All keys documented in a secure local location (NOT committed to git)
Keys ready to be placed in .env file in subsequent setup step
userStories.notes: DrugBank API may require institutional access. For MVP, mock data fallback is acceptable.

userStories.title: Scaffold project directory structure and environment config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the full project directory structure created and environment configured so all subsequent work has the correct file layout. Create directories: config/, skills/ (with 7 agent subdirs: triage/, radiology/, diagnostic/, pharmacy/, monitoring/, documentation/, research/), a2ui/templates/, a2ui/components/, backend/ (with subdirs: agents/, models/, integrations/, tests/), infrastructure/, docs/. Move existing doc files (01-MedAssist-AI-Architecture.md, 02-A2UI-Templates.md, 03-SKILL-Files.md, 04-Project-Setup.md) into docs/. Create .gitignore with entries: .env, __pycache__/, *.pyc, models/, node_modules/, .venv/, *.egg-info/, .DS_Store. Create .env.example with all required env vars from 04-Project-Setup.md. Copy .env.example to .env and fill in actual API keys obtained in US-003. Initialize git repo if not already: git init && git add -A && git commit -m 'Initial project scaffold'.
acceptanceCriteria: All directories exist: config/, skills/{triage,radiology,diagnostic,pharmacy,monitoring,documentation,research}/, a2ui/templates/, a2ui/components/, backend/{agents,models,integrations,tests}/, infrastructure/, docs/
Doc files moved to docs/ directory
.gitignore exists with .env, __pycache__/, *.pyc, models/, node_modules/, .venv/ entries

.env.example exists with all env vars: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MODEL, MEDIMAGEINSIGHT_MODEL_DIR, QDRANT_HOST, QDRANT_PORT, ORTHANC_HOST, ORTHANC_PORT, FHIR_BASE_URL, RXNORM_API_URL, DRUGBANK_API_KEY, PUBMED_API_KEY, NEO4J_URI, NEO4J_USER, NEO4J_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_HOST, POSTGRES_DB, POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, ENCRYPTION_KEY
.env exists with actual API keys filled in (not committed to git)
Git repo initialized with initial commit

userStories.title: Create all SKILL.md files and skill.json for every agent
userStories.description: As a developer, I need SKILL.md and skill.json files for all 7 specialist agents so the OpenClaw skill registry is complete. Copy content from docs/03-SKILL-Files.md. Each SKILL.md must include: # Skill Name, ## When to Use (with trigger keywords), ## Process (numbered steps), ## Models Used, ## A2UI Output Format, ## Safety Rules, ## Example (input/output). Each skill.json must have: name, version (1.0), agent fields. Agents: triage (ESI scoring, red flags, ClinicalBERT+Claude), radiology (image analysis, MedImageInsight+MedGemma), diagnostic (differential dx, MedGemma 27B+Claude), pharmacy (drug interactions, RxNorm+DrugBank), monitoring (MEWS scoring, time-series ML), documentation (SOAP notes, Claude API), research (PubMed search, Claude+PubMed API). After creating, copy all skills to OpenClaw workspace: cp -r skills/* ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ and verify with: openclaw skills list.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/triage/SKILL.md exists with triage assessment skill definition including ESI 1-5 scoring process and red flag checklist
skills/radiology/SKILL.md exists with medical image analysis skill including modality-specific zero-shot label sets
skills/diagnostic/SKILL.md exists with differential diagnosis skill
skills/pharmacy/SKILL.md exists with drug interaction check skill including severity classification (Critical/Major/Moderate/Minor)
skills/monitoring/SKILL.md exists with vital sign monitoring skill including MEWS calculation formula
skills/documentation/SKILL.md exists with clinical documentation skill including SOAP format
skills/research/SKILL.md exists with clinical evidence skill including PubMed search process
All 7 skill.json files exist with name, version, and agent fields
Skills copied to ~/.openclaw/workspace/skills/ and visible via openclaw skills list

userStories.title: Create all A2UI JSONL templates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all 6 A2UI JSONL template files so the agent interfaces can be pushed to OpenClaw Canvas. Copy JSONL content from docs/02-A2UI-Templates.md. Each file must contain surfaceUpdate (component definitions) and beginRendering messages. Templates: triage-dashboard.jsonl (root > header > ESI stats > patient queue > disclaimer), radiology-report.jsonl (split-panel: left=patient+findings+classification, right=evidence+recommendation+actions), drug-alert.jsonl (alert header > drug pair > severity > interaction > evidence > actions), patient-vitals.jsonl (6 vital cards: HR/BP/SpO2/Temp/RR/MEWS > trend > alert > actions), clinical-notes.jsonl (SOAP editable TextFields > ICD-10 codes > finalize/draft/export), evidence-panel.jsonl (search results with title/citation/evidence level/abstract). Test by pushing one template to Canvas: openclaw nodes list to get node-id, then openclaw nodes canvas a2ui push --jsonl a2ui/templates/triage-dashboard.jsonl --node <node-id>.
acceptanceCriteria: a2ui/templates/triage-dashboard.jsonl exists with valid JSONL containing surfaceUpdate and beginRendering
a2ui/templates/radiology-report.jsonl exists with split-panel layout components
a2ui/templates/drug-alert.jsonl exists with drug pair display and severity badge components
a2ui/templates/patient-vitals.jsonl exists with 6 vital sign card components and trend chart
a2ui/templates/clinical-notes.jsonl exists with SOAP TextField components and ICD-10 codes list
a2ui/templates/evidence-panel.jsonl exists with research result card components
All JSONL files are valid JSON lines format (one JSON object per line)
At least one template successfully pushed to OpenClaw Canvas

userStories.title: Create Docker Compose and start local development services
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a docker-compose.yml to run PostgreSQL, Qdrant, Orthanc, and Neo4j locally so the backend can connect to all required data services. Create infrastructure/docker-compose.yml with version 3.9 and services: postgres (image: postgres:16, port 5432, env POSTGRES_DB=medassist POSTGRES_USER=medassist POSTGRES_PASSWORD=dev-password, volume pgdata), qdrant (image: qdrant/qdrant:latest, port 6333, volume qdrant_data), orthanc (image: orthancteam/orthanc:latest, ports 8042 + 4242, volume orthanc_data), neo4j (image: neo4j:5, env NEO4J_AUTH=neo4j/dev-password, ports 7474 + 7687, volume neo4j_data). Define all named volumes at bottom. Start all services: cd infrastructure && docker compose up -d. Verify all containers running: docker compose ps. Verify connectivity: PostgreSQL on localhost:5432, Qdrant on localhost:6333, Orthanc web on localhost:8042, Neo4j browser on localhost:7474.
acceptanceCriteria: infrastructure/docker-compose.yml exists with version 3.9
PostgreSQL 16 service defined with port 5432, medassist database, and persistent volume
Qdrant service defined with port 6333 and persistent volume
Orthanc service defined with ports 8042 and 4242 and persistent volume
Neo4j 5 service defined with ports 7474 and 7687 and persistent volume
All named volumes declared: pgdata, qdrant_data, orthanc_data, neo4j_data
docker compose up -d starts all 4 services without errors
All services are reachable on their respective ports
userStories.notes: Requires Docker Desktop running. First pull may take several minutes.

userStories.title: Create FastAPI backend with config, dependencies, and health endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the FastAPI backend foundation so all agents and endpoints can be built on top. Create backend/requirements.txt with: fastapi, uvicorn[standard], python-dotenv, pydantic, pydantic-settings, httpx, anthropic, qdrant-client, asyncpg, sqlalchemy[asyncio], transformers, torch, Pillow, python-multipart, pytest, pytest-asyncio. Create Python virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && .venv/Scripts/activate (Windows) or source .venv/bin/activate (Unix). Install deps: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt. Create backend/config.py using pydantic-settings BaseSettings to load all env vars from .env (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_MODEL defaulting to claude-sonnet-4-20250514, QDRANT_HOST defaulting to localhost, QDRANT_PORT defaulting to 6333, ORTHANC_HOST defaulting to localhost, ORTHANC_PORT defaulting to 8042, FHIR_BASE_URL, RXNORM_API_URL defaulting to https://rxnav.nlm.nih.gov/REST, DRUGBANK_API_KEY, PUBMED_API_KEY, NEO4J_URI defaulting to bolt://localhost:7687, POSTGRES_HOST defaulting to localhost, POSTGRES_DB defaulting to medassist, POSTGRES_USER defaulting to medassist, POSTGRES_PASSWORD). Create backend/main.py with FastAPI app, CORS middleware (allow all origins for dev), GET /health returning {status: ok, agents_online: 7, version: 2.0}. Create __init__.py files in backend/agents/, backend/models/, backend/integrations/, backend/tests/. Verify: cd backend && uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 and curl http://localhost:8000/health returns 200.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/requirements.txt exists with all listed dependencies including pytest
Python virtual environment created in .venv/ and dependencies installed
backend/config.py exists using pydantic-settings BaseSettings loading all env vars with sensible defaults
backend/main.py exists with FastAPI app, CORS middleware, and GET /health endpoint
GET /health returns JSON {status: 'ok', agents_online: 7, version: '2.0'}
backend/agents/__init__.py, backend/models/__init__.py, backend/integrations/__init__.py, backend/tests/__init__.py all exist
uvicorn starts successfully on port 8000
userStories.notes: torch installation may require specific CUDA version for GPU support. CPU-only is fine for MVP.

userStories.title: Create BaseAgent abstract class
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a BaseAgent abstract class that all specialist agents inherit from, providing a consistent interface. Create backend/agents/base_agent.py with abstract class BaseAgent containing: properties (agent_id: str, name: str, status: str default 'Active', skills: list[str], queue: int default 0, models_used: list[str], color: str hex accent color, icon: str emoji). Abstract methods: async execute_skill(skill_name: str, params: dict) -> dict, async chat(message: str, context: dict) -> AsyncIterator[str] for streaming. Concrete methods: get_info() -> dict returning all agent metadata as dict, log_audit(request: str, model: str, confidence: float, action: str) -> None that prints audit entry (placeholder for DB logging later).
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/base_agent.py exists with BaseAgent abstract class
BaseAgent has all required properties: agent_id, name, status, skills, queue, models_used, color, icon
BaseAgent has abstract methods: execute_skill and chat
BaseAgent has concrete methods: get_info returning metadata dict, log_audit printing audit entry
BaseAgent uses ABC from abc module for abstract method enforcement

userStories.title: Create PostgreSQL database schema and connection utility
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema and connection utility so agents can log audits and store results. Create backend/integrations/database.py with: async get_db_pool() using asyncpg to connect to PostgreSQL (host/db/user/password from config). Create async init_db() that creates tables: audit_log (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(), agent_id VARCHAR(50), skill_name VARCHAR(100), request_summary TEXT, model_used VARCHAR(100), confidence FLOAT, clinician_action VARCHAR(50), clinician_id VARCHAR(50), response_time_ms INT), patients (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, external_id VARCHAR(100) UNIQUE, name VARCHAR(200), age INT, gender VARCHAR(20), medical_history JSONB, allergies JSONB, medications JSONB, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()), triage_assessments (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, patient_id INT REFERENCES patients, esi_score INT, red_flags JSONB, routing VARCHAR(200), reasoning TEXT, confidence FLOAT, clinician_override BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()), radiology_reports (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, patient_id INT REFERENCES patients, modality VARCHAR(100), findings JSONB, similar_cases JSONB, recommendation TEXT, overall_confidence FLOAT, clinician_action VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()). Call init_db() from FastAPI lifespan startup. Verify: start backend with Docker Compose running, check tables created in PostgreSQL.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/integrations/database.py exists with get_db_pool and init_db functions
All 4 tables defined: audit_log, patients, triage_assessments, radiology_reports with correct column types
Database connection uses config values from backend/config.py
init_db is called from FastAPI app lifespan startup event in main.py
Connection pool is properly created and closed on startup/shutdown
Tables are created successfully when backend starts with PostgreSQL running

userStories.title: Create Qdrant vector database client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Qdrant client wrapper so the Radiology Agent can store and search image embeddings. Create backend/integrations/qdrant_client.py with class QdrantService: __init__(host, port) initializing qdrant_client.QdrantClient. Methods: async create_collection(name: str, vector_size: int) creating collection with cosine distance if not exists. async upsert_embedding(collection: str, id: str, vector: list[float], metadata: dict) storing embedding with payload. async search_similar(collection: str, query_vector: list[float], top_k: int = 5) -> list[dict] returning matches with id, score, and payload. Create medical_images collection with vector size matching MedImageInsight embedding dimension (default 512). Initialize QdrantService as singleton from config in main.py startup. Verify: start backend with Docker Compose, confirm collection created in Qdrant.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/integrations/qdrant_client.py exists with QdrantService class
create_collection method creates collection with cosine distance metric
upsert_embedding method stores vector with metadata payload
search_similar method returns top_k results with id, score, and payload
QdrantService is initialized from config during FastAPI startup
medical_images collection created on startup when Qdrant is running

userStories.title: Create ClinicalBERT NER model wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ClinicalBERT wrapper so the Triage Agent can extract medical entities from clinical text. Create backend/models/clinical_bert.py with class ClinicalBERTService: load_model() loading a medical NER model from HuggingFace transformers (use emilyalsentzer/Bio_ClinicalBERT or a medical NER pipeline). Method extract_entities(text: str) -> dict returning: symptoms (list[str]), conditions (list[str]), medications (list[str]), allergies (list[str]), anatomical_locations (list[str]), temporal_indicators (list[str]). If model unavailable, implement fallback using Claude API for entity extraction with a structured prompt. Include error handling for model loading failures.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/models/clinical_bert.py exists with ClinicalBERTService class
load_model method loads Bio_ClinicalBERT or medical NER model from HuggingFace
extract_entities method accepts clinical text and returns dict with symptoms, conditions, medications, allergies, anatomical_locations, temporal_indicators
Fallback to Claude API extraction if model loading fails
Error handling for model loading and inference failures
userStories.notes: First model download may take several minutes. Claude API fallback ensures dev workflow isn't blocked.

userStories.title: Create MedImageInsight model wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a MedImageInsight wrapper so the Radiology Agent can classify medical images and generate embeddings. Create backend/models/medimageinsight.py with class MedImageInsightService: load_model(model_dir: str) loading MedImageInsight 0.61B from local directory or HuggingFace (lion-ai/MedImageInsights). Methods: classify_image(image: PIL.Image, labels: list[str]) -> list[dict] returning zero-shot classification results as [{label, confidence}] sorted by confidence desc. generate_embedding(image: PIL.Image) -> list[float] returning embedding vector. Define modality label sets as class constants: CHEST_XRAY_LABELS = ['normal', 'pneumonia', 'cardiomegaly', 'pleural effusion', 'atelectasis', 'consolidation', 'pneumothorax', 'fracture', 'mass', 'edema'], BRAIN_MRI_LABELS = ['normal', 'tumor/mass', 'acute stroke', 'hemorrhage', 'cerebral atrophy', 'MS lesion', 'hydrocephalus', 'abscess'], DERMATOLOGY_LABELS = ['benign nevus', 'melanoma', 'basal cell carcinoma', 'squamous cell carcinoma', 'actinic keratosis', 'dermatofibroma', 'vascular lesion']. If model unavailable, return mock classification results for development.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/models/medimageinsight.py exists with MedImageInsightService class
load_model method loads MedImageInsight from local dir or HuggingFace
classify_image method returns list of {label, confidence} dicts sorted by confidence
generate_embedding method returns float vector
Modality label sets defined as class constants for chest_xray, brain_mri, dermatology
Fallback mock results available when model is not loaded
userStories.notes: Requires GPU for real inference. Mock mode allows development without GPU.

userStories.title: Create MedGemma model wrapper
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a MedGemma wrapper so agents can generate radiology reports and perform clinical reasoning. Create backend/models/medgemma.py with class MedGemmaService: load_4b_model(device: str) loading MedGemma 4B from HuggingFace (Google). Method generate_report(findings: list[dict], modality: str, patient_info: dict) -> str generating structured radiology report narrative from classification findings. Method clinical_reasoning(prompt: str, context: dict) -> str for general clinical reasoning (placeholder for 27B, uses 4B or Claude API fallback). If model unavailable, implement fallback using Claude API with a radiology report generation prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/models/medgemma.py exists with MedGemmaService class
load_4b_model method loads MedGemma 4B from HuggingFace
generate_report method accepts findings list, modality, patient info and returns report narrative string
clinical_reasoning method accepts prompt and context, returns reasoning string
Fallback to Claude API when model is not available
userStories.notes: MedGemma 4B requires ~8GB GPU VRAM. Claude API fallback ensures development without GPU.

userStories.title: Implement Triage Agent with ESI scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Triage Agent so patients can be assessed with ESI 1-5 scoring, red flag detection, and routing recommendations. Create backend/agents/triage_agent.py with class TriageAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='triage', name='Triage Agent', icon='🚨', color='#ef4444', skills=['esi_scoring','red_flag_detection','patient_routing','emergency_alert'], models_used=['ClinicalBERT','Claude API']. Implement execute_skill dispatching to skill methods. Implement esi_scoring: accept complaint (str), vitals (dict with hr, bp_sys, bp_dia, spo2, temp, rr), pain_scale (int), duration (str), history (str), allergies (list), medications (list). Use ClinicalBERTService to extract entities. Check red flags: cardiac (chest pain, syncope), respiratory (dyspnea, SpO2<90%), neurological (altered consciousness, stroke/FAST+, GCS<9), trauma (hemorrhage, burns>20% BSA), other (anaphylaxis, qSOFA>=2). If any red flag → minimum ESI-2. Use Claude API (anthropic client) to determine final ESI 1-5 with reasoning chain. Return: esi_score, esi_label, red_flags[], routing, wait_time, reasoning, confidence. Implement chat method using Claude with triage-specific system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/triage_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
esi_scoring skill accepts patient data and returns esi_score (1-5), esi_label, red_flags list, routing recommendation, wait_time estimate, reasoning text, and confidence score
Red flag detection covers cardiac, respiratory, neurological, trauma, and other categories
Any detected red flag forces minimum ESI-2
Claude API called for ESI determination with full clinical context
chat method provides streaming responses with triage-specific system prompt
All outputs include disclaimer: 'AI-assisted triage — requires clinician verification'

userStories.title: Implement Radiology Agent with image analysis pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Radiology Agent so medical images can be analyzed with classification, KNN evidence search, and report generation. Create backend/agents/radiology_agent.py with class RadiologyAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='radiology', name='Radiology Agent', icon='🩻', color='#00b4d8', skills=['xray_analysis','mri_interpretation','ct_review','report_gen','evidence_search'], models_used=['MedImageInsight','MedGemma 4B','Qdrant']. Implement xray_analysis: accept image (PIL.Image or bytes), patient_info (dict with name, age, gender). Pipeline: MedImageInsightService.classify_image with CHEST_XRAY_LABELS → generate_embedding → QdrantService.upsert_embedding → QdrantService.search_similar for KNN cases → MedGemmaService.generate_report from findings → safety check (flag confidence < 0.7). Return: findings[] (text, confidence, severity:high/moderate/normal), similar_cases[] (id, similarity_score), recommendation, overall_confidence. Implement evidence_search: query Qdrant by embedding. Implement chat method with radiology system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/radiology_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
xray_analysis skill runs full pipeline: classify → embed → KNN search → generate report
Findings returned with text, confidence score, and severity level (high/moderate/normal)
Similar cases returned from Qdrant KNN with similarity scores
Findings with confidence < 0.7 are flagged for mandatory human review
Report narrative generated by MedGemma (or Claude fallback)
All outputs include disclaimer: 'AI-assisted analysis — requires radiologist review'

userStories.title: Create RxNorm and DrugBank integration clients
userStories.description: As a developer, I need RxNorm and DrugBank API clients so the Pharmacy Agent can resolve drug names and check interactions. Create backend/integrations/rxnorm_client.py with class RxNormClient: resolve_drug_name(name: str) -> dict calling GET https://rxnav.nlm.nih.gov/REST/rxcui.json?name={name} returning CUI + normalized name. get_interactions(rxcui_list: list[str]) -> list[dict] calling GET /interaction/list.json returning pairwise interactions with severity. Create backend/integrations/drugbank_client.py with class DrugBankClient: search_drug(name: str) -> dict searching DrugBank. get_interactions(drugbank_id: str) -> list[dict] returning known interactions. get_contraindications(drugbank_id: str) -> list[dict]. Both clients use httpx.AsyncClient for async HTTP calls and handle rate limiting and errors gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/integrations/rxnorm_client.py exists with RxNormClient class
resolve_drug_name method calls RxNorm REST API and returns CUI + normalized name
get_interactions method returns pairwise drug interactions from RxNorm
backend/integrations/drugbank_client.py exists with DrugBankClient class
Both clients use httpx.AsyncClient for async HTTP
Error handling for API failures and rate limiting

userStories.title: Implement Pharmacy Agent with drug interaction checking
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Pharmacy Agent so drug interactions can be checked with severity classification and workflow blocking. Create backend/agents/pharmacy_agent.py with class PharmacyAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='pharmacy', name='Pharmacy Agent', icon='💊', color='#f59e0b', skills=['drug_interaction','dosage_calc','contraindication','med_reconciliation'], models_used=['Claude API','RxNorm API','DrugBank API']. Implement drug_interaction: accept drug_names (list[str]), patient_id (optional). Resolve each drug to RxNorm CUI via RxNormClient. Query DrugBank for interactions between all pairs. Classify severity: Critical (life-threatening, blocked=true requires physician override), Major (significant risk), Moderate (dose adjustment), Minor (awareness). Return: interactions[] (drug_a, drug_b, severity, description, evidence_source, blocked), alternatives[]. Implement dosage_calc: accept drug, weight, age, renal_function → return dose range. Implement chat with pharmacy system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/pharmacy_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
drug_interaction skill resolves drug names to RxNorm CUIs and checks interactions
Interactions classified by severity: Critical, Major, Moderate, Minor
Critical interactions set blocked=true requiring physician override with reason
Dosage calculation skill accepts patient parameters and returns dose range
Alternatives suggested for blocked interactions
All outputs include drug interaction safety disclaimers

userStories.title: Implement Monitoring Agent with MEWS scoring
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Monitoring Agent so vital signs can be tracked with MEWS early warning scoring and auto-alerts. Create backend/agents/monitoring_agent.py with class MonitoringAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='monitoring', name='Monitoring Agent', icon='📊', color='#a855f7', skills=['vital_tracking','mews_score','anomaly_detection','alert_gen'], models_used=['Time-series ML','Claude API']. Implement mews_score: accept vitals (hr, bp_sys, spo2, temp, rr). Calculate MEWS per standard: HR(<40 or >130=3, 41-50 or 111-130=2, 51-100=0, 101-110=1), BP sys(<70=3, 71-80=2, 81-100=1, 101-199=0, >200=2), RR(<9=2, 9-14=0, 15-20=1, 21-29=2, >29=3), Temp(<35=2, 35-38.4=0, >38.5=2). Return: mews_total, component_scores, alert_level (Normal 0-2, Increased 3-4, Critical 5+). Implement vital_tracking: store vitals with timestamp, maintain 6-hour window. Implement alert_gen: MEWS>=5 or SpO2<90% triggers immediate critical alert. Implement chat with monitoring system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/monitoring_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
MEWS calculation correctly implements standard scoring for HR, BP, RR, Temp
Alert levels: Normal (0-2), Increased concern (3-4), Critical (5+)
SpO2 < 90% triggers immediate critical alert regardless of MEWS score
Vital tracking stores readings with timestamps for trend analysis
MEWS >= 5 auto-generates critical alert for attending physician

userStories.title: Implement Documentation Agent with SOAP notes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Documentation Agent so clinical notes can be auto-generated in SOAP format with ICD-10 coding. Create backend/agents/documentation_agent.py with class DocumentationAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='docs', name='Documentation Agent', icon='📋', color='#06b6d4', skills=['soap_notes','discharge_summary','icd10_coding','referral_letter'], models_used=['Claude API']. Implement soap_notes: accept patient_id, encounter_data (dict with transcript, triage_output, radiology_output, pharmacy_output, monitoring_output). Use Claude API to generate: S (subjective — patient's words, HPI, symptoms), O (objective — vitals, exam, labs, imaging), A (assessment — diagnosis, differentials), P (plan — treatment, meds, follow-up). Auto-suggest ICD-10 codes from Assessment. Return: soap_sections (dict), icd10_codes[] (code, description, confidence), draft_status='pending_review'. Implement discharge_summary and icd10_coding skills. Chat with documentation system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/documentation_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
SOAP notes skill generates all 4 sections (S, O, A, P) from encounter data using Claude API
ICD-10 codes auto-suggested from Assessment section
All notes returned as draft_status='pending_review' requiring clinician approval
Discharge summary skill generates formatted summary from encounter history
All outputs include disclaimer: 'Auto-generated — review and edit before finalizing'

userStories.title: Create PubMed integration client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a PubMed API client so the Research Agent can search medical literature. Create backend/integrations/pubmed_client.py with class PubMedClient: search(query: str, max_results: int = 10, min_date: str = None) -> list[dict] calling NCBI E-utilities: esearch.fcgi to get PMIDs, then efetch.fcgi to get article metadata. Return list of dicts with: pmid, title, authors (list[str]), journal, year, abstract, publication_type. Method fetch_abstracts(pmids: list[str]) -> list[dict] fetching full abstracts for a list of PMIDs. Method format_citation(article: dict) -> str formatting as standard citation string. Use httpx.AsyncClient. Handle NCBI rate limiting (max 3 requests/sec without API key, 10/sec with key).
acceptanceCriteria: backend/integrations/pubmed_client.py exists with PubMedClient class
search method calls NCBI esearch + efetch and returns article metadata
Results include pmid, title, authors, journal, year, abstract, publication_type
fetch_abstracts method retrieves full abstracts for given PMIDs
format_citation method produces standard citation string
Rate limiting handled (respects NCBI API limits)

userStories.title: Implement Research Agent with PubMed search and evidence synthesis
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Research Agent so clinicians can search medical literature and get synthesized evidence. Create backend/agents/research_agent.py with class ResearchAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='research', name='Research Agent', icon='📚', color='#10b981', skills=['guideline_search','evidence_synthesis','trial_match','literature_review'], models_used=['Claude API','PubMed API']. Implement guideline_search: accept clinical_question (str). Use Claude to formulate optimal PubMed search query. Search via PubMedClient. Filter by recency and evidence level (meta-analysis > RCT > cohort > case). Return: results[] (title, authors, journal, year, pmid, abstract, evidence_level), search_query_used. Implement evidence_synthesis: use Claude to synthesize findings from multiple PubMed results into concise summary with citations. Implement trial_match: placeholder for ClinicalTrials.gov search. Chat with research system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/research_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
guideline_search skill formulates PubMed query using Claude, searches, and returns results with evidence levels
evidence_synthesis skill summarizes multiple articles into concise summary with citations
Results always include source citations (PMID, authors, journal, year)
Evidence levels noted for each result (meta-analysis, RCT, cohort, case)
All outputs present as 'evidence suggests...' not as clinical recommendations

userStories.title: Implement Diagnostic Agent with differential diagnosis
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Diagnostic Agent so differential diagnoses can be generated from clinical data. Create backend/agents/diagnostic_agent.py with class DiagnosticAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='diagnostic', name='Diagnostic Agent', icon='🔬', color='#22c55e', skills=['differential_dx','test_recommendation','pattern_recognition','rare_disease'], models_used=['MedGemma 27B','Claude API']. Implement differential_dx: accept symptoms (list[str]), vitals (dict), lab_results (dict optional), imaging_results (dict optional), patient_history (dict). Use Claude API (MedGemma 27B fallback) for clinical reasoning. Return ranked differentials[] (diagnosis, probability, supporting_evidence[], contradicting_evidence[]), recommended_tests[]. Implement test_recommendation: suggest labs/imaging based on differential list. Implement pattern_recognition: identify symptom patterns. Chat with diagnostic system prompt.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/diagnostic_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
differential_dx skill accepts clinical data and returns ranked differential diagnosis list
Each differential includes diagnosis, probability, supporting and contradicting evidence
Recommended tests returned based on differential list
Pattern recognition identifies known symptom-disease patterns
All outputs include disclaimer: 'AI-assisted — requires clinician verification'

userStories.title: Implement Coordinator Agent with routing and consensus
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Coordinator Agent so incoming messages are routed to the correct specialist and complex cases get multi-agent consensus. Create backend/agents/coordinator_agent.py with class CoordinatorAgent(BaseAgent): agent_id='coordinator', name='Coordinator Agent', icon='🧠', color='#e879f9', skills=['agent_routing','consensus','safety_check','escalation'], models_used=['Claude API']. Implement agent_routing: accept message (str), use Claude to analyze intent and determine which specialist agent(s) to invoke. Return: target_agents (list[str]), reasoning (str). Implement consensus: accept agent_results (list[dict]) from multiple agents, analyze agreement/disagreement, build combined report. If agents disagree, present both findings with confidence scores and flag for human review. Implement safety_check: verify confidence > 0.7, check for critical flags (ESI 1-2, critical drug interactions), enforce escalation. Implement escalation: log and trigger alert for attending physician. Register all other agents in coordinator for routing.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/agents/coordinator_agent.py exists inheriting from BaseAgent
agent_routing uses Claude to analyze message and select target specialist agent(s)
consensus method merges results from multiple agents and flags disagreements
safety_check verifies confidence > 0.7 and checks for critical flags
escalation method logs alert for ESI 1-2 and critical findings
Coordinator holds references to all specialist agents for dispatching

userStories.title: Create FHIR R4 EHR integration client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a FHIR client so agents can read patient data from EHR systems. Create backend/integrations/fhir_client.py with class FHIRClient: __init__(base_url: str). Methods: async get_patient(patient_id: str) -> dict fetching Patient resource. async get_conditions(patient_id: str) -> list[dict] fetching active Condition resources. async get_medications(patient_id: str) -> list[dict] fetching active MedicationRequest resources. async get_allergies(patient_id: str) -> list[dict] fetching AllergyIntolerance resources. async get_observations(patient_id: str, category: str) -> list[dict] fetching Observation resources (vitals, labs). Use httpx.AsyncClient. Parse FHIR R4 JSON resources into simplified dicts. Handle connection errors gracefully (return empty results if EHR unavailable).
acceptanceCriteria: backend/integrations/fhir_client.py exists with FHIRClient class
get_patient returns patient demographics (name, age, gender, DOB)
get_conditions returns list of active conditions
get_medications returns list of current medications
get_allergies returns list of allergies/intolerances
get_observations returns vitals or labs filtered by category
Graceful error handling when FHIR server is unavailable

userStories.title: Create Orthanc DICOM integration client
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a DICOM client so the Radiology Agent can receive and retrieve medical images from Orthanc. Create backend/integrations/dicom_client.py with class DicomClient: __init__(host: str, port: int). Methods: async upload_study(dicom_bytes: bytes) -> str uploading DICOM file to Orthanc REST API (POST /instances), returning instance ID. async get_study(study_id: str) -> dict retrieving study metadata from Orthanc. async get_image(instance_id: str) -> PIL.Image retrieving rendered image (GET /instances/{id}/preview) and converting to PIL.Image for model input. async list_studies(patient_id: str) -> list[dict] listing all studies for a patient. Use httpx.AsyncClient for all requests to Orthanc REST API (default port 8042).
acceptanceCriteria: backend/integrations/dicom_client.py exists with DicomClient class
upload_study method uploads DICOM to Orthanc and returns instance ID
get_study returns study metadata
get_image retrieves image and converts to PIL.Image for model inference
list_studies returns all studies for a given patient
Uses httpx.AsyncClient with configurable host and port

userStories.title: Implement all REST API endpoints for agents and skills
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all REST API endpoints wired up so the frontend and external clients can interact with agents. In backend/main.py (or separate router files) add: GET /agents returning list of all 8 agents via get_info(). GET /agents/{id} returning single agent detail. POST /agents/{id}/chat accepting {message, context} and returning streaming SSE response via agent.chat(). POST /agents/{id}/execute accepting {skill_name, params} and returning agent.execute_skill() result. GET /skills listing all registered skills across agents. POST /triage/assess accepting patient data and invoking TriageAgent.execute_skill('esi_scoring', params). POST /radiology/analyze accepting image upload (multipart) and invoking RadiologyAgent.execute_skill('xray_analysis', params). POST /pharmacy/check accepting {drug_names, patient_id} and invoking PharmacyAgent.execute_skill('drug_interaction', params). POST /monitoring/vitals accepting vitals dict and invoking MonitoringAgent.execute_skill('mews_score', params). POST /documentation/generate accepting encounter data and invoking DocumentationAgent.execute_skill('soap_notes', params). POST /research/search accepting {query} and invoking ResearchAgent.execute_skill('guideline_search', params). Initialize all agents on startup. Use APIRouter for clean organization. Verify all endpoints respond correctly with curl or httpie.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /agents returns list of all 8 agents with id, name, status, skills, queue, color, icon
GET /agents/{id} returns single agent detail or 404
POST /agents/{id}/chat streams responses via SSE using agent.chat()
POST /agents/{id}/execute invokes skill and returns structured result
POST /triage/assess invokes triage ESI scoring and returns assessment
POST /radiology/analyze accepts image upload and returns analysis with findings
POST /pharmacy/check returns drug interaction results with severity
POST /monitoring/vitals returns MEWS score and alert level
POST /documentation/generate returns SOAP note sections
POST /research/search returns PubMed results with evidence levels
All domain endpoints return proper error responses for invalid input

userStories.title: Integrate frontend React dashboard with backend API
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the existing React dashboard connected to the real backend API so mock data is replaced with live data. Update medassist-agent-interface.jsx (or copy to frontend/src/components/MedAssistDashboard.jsx if frontend is scaffolded): Replace the static agents array with a useEffect that fetches from GET /agents on mount. Replace triagePatients with fetch from GET /triage/assess or a new GET /triage/queue endpoint. Replace radiologyReport with fetch from a reports endpoint. Replace vitals with fetch from GET /monitoring/vitals. Update handleSend in Agent Chat to POST to /agents/{id}/chat and stream SSE responses instead of setTimeout mock. Add API base URL config (default http://localhost:8000). Keep the existing UI design, styling, and layout unchanged. Add error states for when backend is unreachable.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent dashboard fetches agent list from GET /agents API on mount
Triage view fetches patient queue from backend instead of static mock data
Radiology view fetches report data from backend
Vitals view fetches live vital signs from backend
Agent Chat sends messages to POST /agents/{id}/chat and renders streamed responses
API base URL is configurable (default http://localhost:8000)
Error states shown when backend is unreachable
Existing UI design, colors, and layout preserved

userStories.title: Create clinical scenario end-to-end tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need end-to-end tests for critical clinical scenarios so I can verify the system works correctly. Create backend/tests/test_scenarios.py using pytest + httpx.AsyncClient. Scenario 1 (ESI-1 triage): POST /triage/assess with 67F, crushing chest pain, BP 90/60, HR 110, SpO2 94% → assert esi_score=1, red_flags not empty, routing contains 'resuscitation'. Scenario 2 (ESI-3 triage): POST /triage/assess with 32F, abdominal pain, fever 39.2C → assert esi_score=3. Scenario 3 (Drug interaction): POST /pharmacy/check with ['warfarin','ibuprofen'] → assert severity='Critical', blocked=true. Scenario 4 (MEWS critical): POST /monitoring/vitals with HR=135, BP sys=80, RR=32, Temp=39.5 → assert mews >= 5, alert_level='Critical'. Scenario 5 (Radiology): POST /radiology/analyze with sample image → assert findings list not empty, each finding has confidence score. Scenario 6 (Agent routing): POST /agents/coordinator/chat with 'check drug interaction between warfarin and aspirin' → assert routes to pharmacy agent. Scenario 7 (SOAP note): POST /documentation/generate with mock encounter → assert all 4 SOAP sections present. Run tests: cd backend && pytest tests/test_scenarios.py -v.
acceptanceCriteria: backend/tests/test_scenarios.py exists with at least 7 test scenarios
ESI-1 scenario verifies correct triage scoring for life-threatening presentation
Drug interaction scenario verifies Critical severity and workflow blocking
MEWS scenario verifies correct score calculation and critical alert
Radiology scenario verifies findings with confidence scores returned
Agent routing scenario verifies coordinator routes to correct specialist
SOAP note scenario verifies all 4 sections generated
Tests use pytest and httpx.AsyncClient against running FastAPI app
pytest tests/test_scenarios.py -v runs without import errors
userStories.notes: Requires backend running with Docker Compose services up. Some tests need Claude API key.
